chapter_title: About Attending the Prayer and the Circles of

hadith_entries.text:
334. Narrated Abu Qatada: 'While we were praying
with the Prophet he heard the noise of some people. After
performing the prayer he said: "What was the problem?"
They replied; 'We were hurrying for the prayer." He said:
"Do not make haste for the prayer, and whenever you come
for the prayer, you should come with calmness, and pray
whatever you get (with the people) and complete the rest that
you have missed." (Bukhari)
Riyadhos-Saliheen (The Meadows of the Righteous)

335. Narrated Jaber: "The Prophet spurred his riding
animal in Mohassir valley." The narration of Bishr adds the
following words: "Then he proceeded from Jam'a in tranquility
and ordered them to be in tranquility.' The narration of Abu-
No'aym adds the following words: "And he (the Prophet)
ordered them to throw the pebbles equal to the beans' seed and
said: 'I might not meet you after this year of mine. ""
Allah said:
*
